citations.text: The Free Times has called us "Especially Ambitious" but I think we can handle it. Saturday 31 New Brookland Tavern Covers Eve — If it ain’t broke, why fix it? The venerable New Brookland Tavern has for years offered New Year’s Eve as an opportunity for regular guests to its stage to pay tribute to their favorite acts and biggest influences. This year’s no different, with a bevy of local musician...s banding together to do some one-off tributeering; we’re most excited about muscular indie-pop act Ye Mighty! tackling Queen, mostly to hear how it’ll pull off “Bohemian Rhapsody.” Also on the bill: Versus the Robot as Green Day; The Noise as Faith No More; Philadelphia-based punk act Big Attack! as Elvis Costello and the Attractions (!!!); and Genius of the Heard as — and this one’s especially ambitious — Talking Heads. P. Wall New Brookland Tavern: 7 p.m., $5 ($8 under 21); 791-4413, newbrooklandtavern.com.

citations.text: Random Survey: 1. Do you like Bleu Cheese? As a friend 2. Coke or Pepsi? I've never done Pepsi, is it legal? 3. Do you own a gun? 'merica 4. What do you think of hot dogs? Bring them inside, let them cool off a little, or throw them in the pool. 5. Favorite cookie? Fortune 6. Favorite TV show? Cop Rock 7. Favorite movie? Chud 9. Can you do a pushup? I don't wear a bra anymore 10. Favorite jewelry? All Jewelry matters 11. Favorite hobby? Hollie 12. Do you have A.D.D.? I'm gonna come back to this one later 13. Do you wear glasses? Only when I need to see something 14. What book are you currently reading? "Lose Weight by Eating Skinny People" by Dick Handler 15. Three things you did yesterday? Your mom, your sister, your girlfriend. 16. Three things you drink regularly? None, I'm an irregular drinker. 17. Dogs or cats? Depends on how you cook them. 18. Current dislike? Same as past dislike. 19. Favorite place to go? Big Bone Lick, KY 20. How did you bring in the New year? passed out facedown by the hostess stand at Spaghetti Warehouse. 21. Where would you like to go? Arby's 22. Name five people who will do this: B.J Cobbledick, Dixie Normous, Phat Ho, Jack Kanoff, Luedglenristen Kleisswegaard Wolfenstien Smith 23. Favorite color? Corn nut 24. Do you like sleeping on satin sheets? yeah, I love randomly sliding out of bed at 3am. 25. Can you whistle? I've never tried it. 26. Where are you now? North Piddle 27. Would you be a pirate? What are the hours? Are weekends involved? 28. Favorite food? Jellied Moose Nose 29. Last thing that made you laugh? Politics 30. Favorite animal? Harvey Weinstien 31. Favorite song on the radio currently? To answer this, I'd have to turn on the radio, listen to every song that's currently on every station, and if any of those songs ended while I was deciding, I'd have to listen to them and mark the ones that currently aren't on anymore. And while I was doing this, more songs would end and new songs would come on. It's too much trouble. I'm not willing to go through it. I probably wouldn't like any of them. 32. How many countries have you visited? 69 33. Best smell? Napalm in the morning 34. Do you like to dance? Not with you 35. How many siblings do you have? I don't have them, they have me. 36.Do you love life? yeah, but I'm not willing to get into a serious relationship with it right now.

citations.text: Stacy Murphy Ross - It took me a while to get to it, but here you go. "Rules of the Game: Copy this and post as your status update. List 12 albums that stayed with you, but only 1 per band/artist. Don't take too long and don't think too hard. Tag 12 (or less, or more) friends to do the same, including me, so I can see what you put. No compilations/Greatest Hits. Reasons or memories help us see your favorites the way you do, so share a little about why it made your list! Feel free to play along even if you weren't tagged. 1. AC/DC - Dirty Deeds Done Dirt Cheap - First album I bought with my own money. 2. Beatles - White Album - It blew my 8 year old mind 3. Johnny Cash - Everybody Loves a Nut - Self explanatory. 4. Rainmakers - The Rainmakers - Music and sarcasm swirled together. 5. Uncle Tupelo - Anodyne - When I was in the Navy, I'd take several CD's with me every time we went to sea, this was the only one I listened to for and entire 3 month patrol. Bands that are falling apart make great albums. (Queensrych - Empire was another good 'Patrol album'. 6. The Clash - London Calling - I couldn't get my friends to buy into the Clash, but I wore this album out anyway. 7. Drive-by Truckers- The Dirty South - This was the best lineup for the DBT's in my correct opinion. 8. Freddy King - Is a Blues Master - Freddy paved the way for SRV. Gerald Jemmott plays bass on this album which made it maximum awesome 9. Eagles - On The Border - It's not cool to love the Eagles, but I do it anyway. 10. Tesla - Mechanical Resonance - I still jam to this album. 11. Waylon Jennings, Willie Nelson, Jessi Colter, and Tompall Glaser - Wanted! The Outlaws - Technical this is a cheat because it's a compilation album, but it's so much more. 12. Weird Al" Yankovic in 3-D - My little brother gave me this album and it reminds me of him anytime I hear "Eat It" (or "Beat it" too).

citations.text: Yesterday someone told me I should be a writer for Saturday Night Fever. This seemed odd, but I thought I'd give it a shot. I wrote a screenplay for Saturday Night Fever II - Tony Takes Manhattan!. Then I discovered there already WAS a Saturday Night Fever II (Staying Alive) AND Saturday Night Fever III (Urban Cowboy). So I wrote a screenplay for :Saturday Night Fever IV - Geriatric Boga-loo." Keeping the continuity of the other 3 movies was difficult since I haven't seen any of them but I think I did a good job. I gave the screenplay to my friend today and he said "NO, I said SATURDAY NIGHT LIVE". Well son of a bitch, now I'm out of ideas.
